claim: Residence-window summaries can expose time-in-state behavior that amplitude summaries miss.
Bounded-coupling decisions require predeclared margins, uncertainty intervals, and ROPE or interval support when supplied.
Reserve-like summaries can be integrated when the readout is scoped to the measured endpoint.
Reduced-architecture comparison can expose when simpler endpoint mappings do not satisfy routed-output constraints.
The current RhoDyn release can reproduce retained Stage 7 evidence from a source distribution and expose user-facing export provenance.
